nodes.label: Requirement: 30x30 maze, 16 per cell, one entry + one exit
nodes.rationale: Fixed 30x30 grid with a single entrance and single exit defines the playfield scope. Spec says 16 cm per cell; the code interprets this as 16 px per cell (unit discrepancy).

nodes.label: Requirement: entry/exit mutually reachable, >=5 computations
nodes.rationale: Spec demands the two points be provably connected and that at least 5 'computations' occur when placing them. Code satisfies this by BFS-ing >=5 random border pairs and keeping the pair with the longest shortest path, which both proves connectivity and maximizes challenge.

nodes.label: Requirement: mouse player sprite <=16x16, moves 1 cell per press
nodes.rationale: Player is a mouse no larger than one cell (spec says 16x16 cm, code uses 16x16 px), stepping exactly one cell per key press so movement stays grid-aligned.

nodes.label: Requirement: 3-minute limit -> GAME OVER + restart
nodes.rationale: Walking for more than 3 minutes triggers a game over screen followed by an automatic restart, keeping rounds short.

nodes.label: Requirement: cheese at finish, shortest path shown as scent light
nodes.rationale: The mouse always 'knows' its shortest path to the cheese, visualized as a light resembling cheese smell. The code approximates this with a straight glowing beacon line from mouse to cheese rather than rendering the actual BFS path cells — a simplification of the spec's intent.

nodes.label: Requirement: interface like terminal snake game
nodes.rationale: Look and feel modeled on a terminal snake game: the code realizes this as black background, green walls, and a Consolas monospace font.
